perturbation.original_text: XI. Term This Non -Disclosure Agreement shall expire on three (3) calendar years from December 31 of the year in which you execute this No n-Disclosure Agreement; provided, however, that notwithstanding the termination of this Non -Disclosure Agreement, the confidentiality obligations and covenants of this Non -Disclosure Agreement will survive indefinitely and shall not terminate, or else shal l remain in effect as long as permitted by law. GTC may revoke your access to Confidential Information at any time, with or without notice . GTC may extend the term of this Non -Disclosure Agreement in its sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text:
XII. Other Provisions If any pa rt of the Agreement is found to be invalid or unenforceable, the rest of the Agreement will still remain effective. GTC may exercise its rights under this Agreement at any time, even if they delayed doing so or failed to do so in the past. All Confidentia l Information is provided “as is” and with all faults . GTC shall not be liable for the accuracy or completeness of Confidential Information. GTC is not responsible for Recipient’s use of Confidential Information . Recipient agrees to hold GTC harmless an d indemnify them for any and all damages and liabilities arising from any action you may take with respect to Confidential Information. Recipient acknowledge s that GTC is not responsible or liable for any other entity’s designation of information as CEII, including any improper designation.
XI. Term This Non -Disclosure Agreement shall expire on three (3) calendar years from December 31 of the year in which you execute this No n-Disclosure Agreement; provided, however, that notwithstanding the termination of this Non -Disclosure Agreement, the confidentiality obligations and covenants of this Non -Disclosure Agreement will survive indefinitely and shall not terminate, or else shal l remain in effect as long as permitted by law. GTC may revoke your access to Confidential Information at any time, with or without notice . GTC may extend the term of this Non -Disclosure Agreement in its sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The NDA's 'Term' section outlines how long the agreement lasts. By moving this to the 'Other Provisions' section, its importance is downplayed and could be overlooked, especially concerning the indefinite survival of confidentiality obligations. This could lead to confusion about the agreement's duration.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract terms, including duration, should be clear and unambiguous. Moving this section obscures the term's importance.
perturbation.law_explanation: According to Georgia law, contracts should be interpreted to ascertain the intention of the parties, and the language used is paramount. By burying the 'Term' section in 'Other Provisions', there is the risk that a court could find that the intentions were not clear regarding the NDA's term, especially given the clause about the indefinite survival of the confidentiality obligations. The second link requires a Lexis subscription.

Construction of contracts by courts generally; findings of fact by juries The construction of a contract is a question of law for the court. Where any matter of fact is involved, the jury should find the fact. Disclaimer: These codes may not be the most recent version. Georgia may have more current or accurate information. perturbation.justification: The changed text buries the 'Term' section, which defines the agreement's duration and the indefinite survival of confidentiality obligations, within 'Other Provisions'. This structural flaw contradicts Georgia law, which requires contracts to be interpreted according to the intention of the parties as expressed in the language used, as noted in Scraped Law Snippet 1: 'The construction of a contract is a question of law for the court.' By obscuring the 'Term' section, the intention regarding the NDA's term becomes unclear, creating a contradiction because the organization of the contract affects its clarity and enforceability.
